spec.title: Spec audit templates and finding schema

humanSummary.text: Example report with one finding at each severity. The P0 and P1 findings block completion; the P2 and P3 findings are machine-convertible follow-up work.

findings.title: Audit output is not machine-parseable JSON
findings.description: The implementation does not provide a schema or validator path for audit reports, so orchestration cannot ingest audit output deterministically.

evidence.detail: No audit report schema is available for the orchestrator to compile.

findings.impact: Agents could mark a spec complete from prose-only audit output, losing blocking findings.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A JSON Schema for audit reports exists under roadmap/ and rejects unknown top-level and finding fields.
just roadmap-validate validates at least one audit report fixture against the schema.

findings.title: Findings can omit actionable acceptance criteria
findings.description: A finding without explicit repair criteria is not actionable by the implementation worker.

evidence.detail: The audit template does not require actionable acceptance criteria for every finding.

findings.impact: The owning spec can be blocked by a finding that gives no objective completion condition.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Every finding requires one or more actionableAcceptanceCriteria strings.
The playbook tells reviewers to downgrade or omit findings that cannot state acceptance criteria.

findings.title: Audit report ingestion command is not yet automated
findings.description: The roadmap validator checks committed examples, but there is no dedicated command for ingesting an arbitrary audit report into node draft output.

evidence.detail: The current command surface validates repository fixtures only.

findings.impact: Future orchestrator automation will need a small ingestion command before it can convert external audit files without custom glue.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A command accepts an audit report path and emits proposed DAG node JSON for P2/P3 findings.
The command rejects reports with blocking P0/P1 findings unless run in validation-only mode.

proposedDagNode.title: Audit report ingestion command
proposedDagNode.summary: Add an orchestrator-facing command that converts validated P2/P3 audit findings into draft DAG node JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: The command validates an input audit report before conversion.
Each P2/P3 finding with draft_new_dag_node produces a planned node draft with acceptance criteria and verification.
auditFocus: Blocking findings are not silently converted into follow-up work

findings.title: Reviewer examples could include more edge cases
findings.description: The playbook could eventually include more examples for downgrade decisions and duplicate findings.

evidence.detail: The first template focuses on canonical P0-P3 cases.

findings.impact: Reviewer training may take longer, but this does not affect alpha readiness.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Add examples for duplicate findings, accepted risk, and severity downgrades.
State when a note should remain prose-only instead of becoming a finding.

acceptanceCriteria: Audit ingestion fixture review covers duplicate finding and severity downgrade cases.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Illustrative P3 example for conversion into a different existing planned node.
